Feature Specification: Graph and Context Optimization
Level 3 coordination packet for the v2 cross-phase synthesis, adoption decisions, and downstream sequencing for graph and context optimization in Public.
Deliver an evidence-anchored recommendation set for reducing Claude Code token spend in Code_Environment/Public, derived from auditing a primary-source Reddit field report covering 858-926 sessions.

Read-only 20-iteration research investigation of the codesight external Node.js/TypeScript skill covering detector discipline, MCP/cache semantics, profile generation safety, scanner heuristics, and adoption patterns Public should adopt, adapt, or reject.

Research Contextador's MCP query architecture, self-healing feedback loop, and Mainframe shared cache to identify concrete, evidence-backed improvements for Code_Environment/Public's retrieval surfaces.

Read-only research investigation of the graphify external Python skill to identify two-pass AST+LLM extraction, Leiden clustering, evidence-tagging, multimodal, and PreToolUse hook patterns Public should adopt, adapt, or reject.

Read-only 20-iteration research investigation of the Claudest external Claude Code plugin marketplace and the claude-memory plugin (FTS5/BM25 conversation recall, SessionStart context injection, extract-learnings consolidation, get-token-insights observability) to identify adopt/prototype/reject patterns and execution-ready follow-on packet contracts for Code_Environment/Public's Spec Kit Memory and Code Graph stack.
Feature Specification: Research Memory Redundancy Follow-On
Research-aligned rescope of packet 002 from a six-phase warning prototype to a bounded producer-side continuity prerequisite packet.
Parent packet spec for the shipped remediation train plus the scoped Phase 8 and Phase 9 follow-ons triggered by later packet audits.

Phase 1 defines the P0 foundation slice for memory-quality remediation: fix the OVERVIEW anchor mismatch, introduce the shared truncation helper, and preserve OVERVIEW text on word boundaries before later metadata and heuristic phases build on it.
Phase 2 specifies the P1 metadata fixes for D4 importance-tier drift and D7 empty git provenance, using the parent research packet as the sole planning authority.
Phase 3 of the memory-quality remediation train scopes PR-5 and PR-6 only: trigger-phrase sanitization plus a precedence-only gate for decision extraction.
Phase 4 of the memory quality remediation train: D5 auto-supersedes with continuation gating, SaveMode enum refactor, and post-save reviewer CHECK-D1..D8 coverage.
Feature Specification: Phase 5 — Operations & Tail PRs
Phase 6 defines the narrowed implementation-owner follow-on that turns generated memory saves into compact retrieval wrappers while restoring canonical ownership to packet docs.
Phase 7 defines the downstream audit and update pass that checks whether Phase 1-6 changes require parity updates across docs, commands, templates, MCP surfaces, and agent definitions.
Feature Specification: Input Normalizer Fast-Path Fix
Level 3 sub-phase for nine render-layer fixes in the memory-save pipeline so compact-wrapper saves match the 006 wrapper contract.
Level 3 packet for closing the remaining memory-save schema, sanitizer, validator, and D5 continuation defects identified by the 2026-04-09 RCA and skipped-recommendation audits.

Phase 4 defines the execution-guardrail update for three AGENTS files so the moved request-analysis block under Critical Rules is leaner, later sections remain renumbered cleanly, and agents keep the same operational execution behavior without duplicate scaffolding.
Feature Specification: Phase 6 — Continuity Refactor Gates
Remove known template and packet-prep blockers before Gate B schema and runtime work begins.

Establish the irreversible foundation for phase 018 by rehearsing the migration on a copy, adding the approved causal-edge anchor fields, executing the rebaselined archive flip, and preserving the post-cleanup compatibility contract before writer and reader retargeting begins.
Gate C defines the writer-critical path for phase 018: validator, routed save path, merge engine, template continuity rollout, and parity-plus-rollback proof readiness.
Retarget the phase 018 reader surfaces onto canonical spec docs and thin continuity records, then prove the new resume ladder with regression and latency gates.
Feature Specification: Gate E — Runtime Migration
Verify Gate B cleanup completeness, confirm no orphan references remain, and record whether archived-tier runtime cleanup is already complete or still needs follow-up.

Feature specification for the `/memory:save` planner-first default contract. This packet rewrites the save path so plan-only becomes the honest default, legacy `[spec]/memory/*.md` behavior is retired end-to-end, full-auto mutation is preserved only as an explicit fallback, and metadata freshness, enrichment, and indexing work move behind callable follow-up APIs rather than riding the default save path by accident.

Remediation specification for 243 deduplicated findings synthesized from the merged 120-iteration deep review across packets 009, 010, 012, and 014. The packet is organized around 11 workstreams, with the governed save-time reconsolidation scope leak treated as the release-blocking P0, followed by sequential hardening workstreams and then batch-automatable correctness, security, and test-quality remediations once the scope-boundary foundations are in place.
Feature Specification: Foundational Runtime Remediation (Phase 016 → Phase 016 remediation Charter)

Remediation charter for 27 consolidated tasks closing the Phase 017 review backlog across four waves. The headline fix is H-56-1: the canonical `/memory:save` path had been a deterministic metadata-freshness no-op because of a dead-code guard, a full-auto-only graph refresh gate, and the default plan-only execution mode; the packet also closes the code-graph readiness asymmetry, the Copilot compact-cache observability gap, the session-resume auth binding work, and the deferred maintainability follow-ups that Phase 018 depends on for traceable recovery and rollout hygiene.

Phase 017 Wave A child packet: infrastructure primitives (~20h, CRITICAL PATH). 5 tasks: T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04 (canonical save metadata writer, merged PR, 6h, eliminates H-56-1 root cause), T-CGC-01 (extract lib/code-graph/readiness-contract.ts, 4h, prereq for Wave B T-W1-CGC-03), T-W1-HOK-02 (extract hooks/shared-provenance.ts, 4h, prereq for Wave B T-W1-HOK-01 — breaks Gemini→Claude transitive import R52-P2-001), T-SCP-01 (collapse 4 local normalizers to canonical, 4h, resolves R1-P1-001 + R4-P1-001 compound), T-EVD-01-prep (rewrap 016 checklist.md evidence markers ')' → ']', 2h, prereq for Wave C T-EVD-01 strict mode). Blocks all downstream Wave B/C/D work. Atomic-ship: G1 (T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04) MUST land as single PR. Gate: /spec_kit:deep-review :auto ×7 emits ZERO new P0/P1.

Phase 017 Wave B child packet: 9 tasks / 30h across 3 parallel lanes consuming Wave A primitives. Lane B1 (Cluster B consumers, 12h, 4 tasks): T-CNS-02 research folder backfill, T-W1-CNS-05 continuity freshness validator, T-CGC-02 context.ts explicit error branch, T-RBD-03 design-intent comments. Lane B2 (Cluster D + Cluster E, 16h, 2 tasks): T-W1-CGC-03 5-sibling code-graph readiness propagation (atomic-ship or stub rollout), T-W1-HOK-01 Copilot compact-cache + session-prime (depends Wave A T-W1-HOK-02). Lane B3 (Cluster A + C + Standalone P1, 12h, 3 tasks): T-SCP-02 normalizer lint rule, T-SAN-01 + T-SAN-02 + T-SAN-03 NFKC unicode normalization + tests, T-PIN-RET-01 retry-exhaustion counter. Resolves FC-3 (research iteration folders metadata), FC-4 (6 code-graph handlers readiness vocabulary), FC-5 (Copilot compact-cache wiring), FC-6 (NFKC Gate 3 hardening), FC-8 (partial_causal_link_unresolved retry-exhaustion). Wave B gate: /spec_kit:deep-review :auto ×7 + validate.sh --strict on 017 folder.

Phase 017 Wave C child: 5 tree-wide rollout and sweep tasks (~15h / 2 days). Activates evidence-marker lint in strict mode (T-EVD-01), runs 16-folder canonical-save sweep with per-folder commits (T-CNS-03), amends stale closing-pass-notes CP-002 (T-CPN-01), renames memory-context `readiness` field to `advisoryPreset` (T-W1-MCX-01), and binds session-resume auth to MCP transport identity with permissive-mode canary (T-SRS-BND-01). HIGH rollback cost on 16-folder sweep mitigated by 1-folder smoke test and pre-sweep git stash. Depends on Wave A (`../001-infrastructure-primitives/`) and Wave B (`../002-cluster-consumers/`) merged; blocks Wave D (`../004-p2-maintainability/`) ship gate.
Spec: Phase 017 Wave D — P2 Maintainability (Deferrable)
